steps.title: Observe the structure of the equation
steps.content: We have $$\frac{x+5}{3}=\frac{x+5}{12}$$. The numerator $(x+5)$ appears in both fractions, but the denominators are different: 3 and 12.

steps.title: Apply the equality principle
steps.content: For two fractions to be equal when they have the same numerator but different denominators, the numerator must equal zero. If the numerator were non-zero, dividing it by 3 would give a different result than dividing it by 12, contradicting the equation. Therefore, $x+5 = 0$.

steps.title: Find the interval containing x+5
steps.content:
We've determined that $x+5 = 0$. Now identify which interval contains this value:
  • Choice A: between 2 and 6 — does not contain 0
  • Choice B: between −2 and 2 — contains 0
  • Choice C: between −6 and −3 — does not contain 0
  • Choice D: between 7 and 12 — does not contain 0
That matches choice B — B is correct.
